zh.lifeSummary: 當Marie Jeanne de  Picquigny於1305年出生在法國豪-德-福朗斯索姆沙吉時，她的父親Jean De Picquigny II   Seigneur de Saint-Ouen26歲，母親Marthe Jeanne d'Amiens27歲。她於1354年與Henri DE BEURES結婚。她去世於1373年在法國享年68歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Marie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Maria . When first introduced to England in the Middle Ages, it was Anglicized in pronunciation and respelled Mary . This French form was reintroduced into the English-speaking world as a separate name in the 19th century, and is still pronounced more or less in the French manner, although sometimes with the stress on the first syllable. It is now often used in combination with other names such as Ellie, Chloe, and Lisa.

nameMeanings.name: Jeanne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Marie Jeanne de  Picquigny was born in 1305, in Saint-Ouen, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France, her father, Jean De Picquigny II   Seigneur de Saint-Ouen, was 26 and her mother, Marthe Jeanne d'Amiens, was 27. She married Henri DE BEURES in 1354. She died in 1373, in France, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Marie Jeanne de  Picquigny est née en 1305, à Saint-Ouen, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France, son père, Jean De Picquigny II   Seigneur de Saint-Ouen, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Marthe Jeanne d'Amiens, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Henri DE BEURES en 1354. Elle est décédée en 1373, à France, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Marie Jeanne de  Picquigny 1305 in Saint-Ouen, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Jean De Picquigny II   Seigneur de Saint-Ouen 26 und ihre Mutter Marthe Jeanne d'Amiens 27. Sie hat 1354 Henri DE BEURES geheiratet. Sie starb 1373 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Marie Jeanne de  Picquigny è nata il nel 1305 nel Saint-Ouen, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia, sua padre, Jean De Picquigny II   Seigneur de Saint-Ouen, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Marthe Jeanne d'Amiens, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato Henri DE BEURES nel 1354. Morì nel 1373, nel Francia, all'età di 68 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Marie Jeanne de  Picquignyが1305年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスソンメサイント-オウンで生まれた時，父Jean De Picquigny II   Seigneur de Saint-Ouenは26歳，母Marthe Jeanne d'Amiensは27歳であった。1354年にHenri DE BEURESと結婚した。彼女は1373年にフランスで68歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Marie Jeanne de  Picquigny(이)가 1305년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 솜 세인트-우언에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Jean De Picquigny II   Seigneur de Saint-Ouen(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marthe Jeanne d'Amiens(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1354년 Henri DE BEURES(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1373년 프랑스에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Marie Jeanne de  Picquigny nasceu em 1305, em Saint-Ouen, Somme, Hauts-de-France, França, seu pai, Jean De Picquigny II   Seigneur de Saint-Ouen, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Marthe Jeanne d'Amiens, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Henri DE BEURES em 1354. Ela faleceu em 1373, em França, com 68 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Marie Jeanne de  Picquigny родилась в 1305 г. в местности сеинт-оуен, сомм, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция, её отцу по имени Jean De Picquigny II   Seigneur de Saint-Ouen было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Marthe Jeanne d'Amiens, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henri DE BEURES в 1354 г. Она умерла в 1373 г. в местности Франция в возрасте 68 лет.
